subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral + independent (IMF, World Bank, FATF, OC Index, Freedom House, RSF, Afrobarometer) + international press.
Africa's highest-income, strongly-governed democracy - the Oct 2025 runoff produced a second peaceful turnover in five years (Herminie unseated incumbent Ramkalawan).

Standout features: strong governance and macro management (~5.8% growth, sub-2% inflation, debt cut to ~54% under an IMF EFF/RSF), against the world's highest per-capita heroin dependency (~10% of working-age), tourism/tuna concentration, and acute climate/sea-level exposure. Net positive.

economic.notes: Strong fiscal consolidation and low inflation on a floating rupee, against tourism/tuna concentration.

fiscal_state.notes: Strong post-COVID consolidation - a ~2.5% primary surplus, debt down to ~53.6% of GDP, all IMF targets met. (IMF EFF/RSF reviews 2025-26)

monetary_independence.notes: Genuine autonomy via the freely floating rupee (floated 2008), though a tiny, highly open economy limits effective independence. (Central Bank of Seychelles)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The rupee is not a reserve currency. (definitional)

banking_stability.notes: Sound and well-capitalized with declining NPLs; off the FATF grey list. (CBS Financial Stability Report 2024; FATF)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Extreme concentration in tourism (>55-70% of GDP/FX) and tuna, highly exposed to external shocks. (World Bank)

institutional.notes: Strong-for-region rule of law, credible data, a capable state, and a free press.

rule_of_law.notes: Strong for the region - independent courts oversaw clean, contested elections and orderly handovers. (Freedom House; 2025 runoff)

statistical_integrity.notes: Credible official statistics, fully IMF-engaged with met benchmarks. (IMF)

civil_service_capacity.notes: A capable administration delivering Africa's top human-development outcomes, modest for a microstate. (World Bank)

press_freedom.notes: Among the freer media environments in Africa, with pluralistic outlets. (RSF; Freedom House)

political_social.notes: A consolidated democracy with two recent peaceful turnovers, eroded socially by addiction.

political_stability.notes: Two peaceful turnovers in five years (2020, 2025) confirm a consolidated, resilient democracy. (Al Jazeera 2025; Africa Center)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Generally fair; reliant on migrant labour with limited reported systemic abuse. (inference)

social_cohesion.notes: Materially eroded by the heroin epidemic (~10% of working-age dependent) and cost-of-living strain - top 2025 voter concerns. (Afrobarometer 2025; ADF)

demographics.notes: Stable, small (~100k) and highly urbanized, with drug dependency a structural workforce drag. (World Bank; OC Index)

geopolitical.notes: A stable, non-aligned partner balancing India, the Gulf, and the West, conflict-free.

alliance_reliability.notes: A stable, non-aligned partner balancing India, Gulf, and Western ties. (observed)

conflict_involvement.notes: No armed conflict; the late-2000s Somali-piracy threat has receded, though it remains on a maritime trafficking route. (OC Index)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: No sanctions exposure, open account, cleared of FATF strategic deficiencies. (FATF)

physical_practical.notes: Among Africa's best healthcare and infrastructure, against acute climate/sea-level and drug-driven crime.

climate.notes: An Indian-Ocean SIDS with sea-level/reef/coastal exposure, but the highest-income African state with relatively strong readiness. (ND-GAIN; IPCC SIDS)

healthcare.notes: Among Africa's best universal systems, but heavily burdened by the addiction crisis (a large methadone caseload). (WHO; Afrobarometer 2025)

infrastructure.notes: Good for the region, supporting a high-end tourism economy. (World Bank)

crime_safety.notes: Elevated drug-driven crime (drug offences +41% 2022-23) despite low political violence. (OC Index)

disaster_insurance.notes: High climate exposure with limited penetration, partly offset by stronger fiscal headroom than peers. (inference; IMF)

summary: Africa's income and human-development leader and a genuinely consolidated democracy, underscored by a second peaceful turnover in five years when Patrick Herminie unseated incumbent Ramkalawan in the October 2025 runoff. Macro management is strong - ~5.8% growth on record tourism, inflation under 2%, and debt cut to ~54% of GDP under an on-track IMF EFF/RSF. The defining negatives are social and physical, not institutional: the world's highest per-capita heroin dependency drives crime and strains cohesion, while its tourism-and-tuna economy is acutely exposed to external shocks and climate/sea-level risk. Net skew is clearly positive.
